structuralIssues.title: Four content sections empty
structuralIssues.description: content.standards_landscape, content.standards_development, content.key_risks, and content.sources_summary are empty.
structuralIssues.action_required: Populate with proposed content from deep-dive report §8.

structuralIssues.title: Big-3 all publish EN 15804 EPDs — strong DPP readiness among market leaders
structuralIssues.description: Knauf publishes EN 15804+A2 EPDs via EPD International (GKB, GKI, A-ZERO). Saint-Gobain publishes via Gyproc/British Gypsum brands, IBU-verified. British Gypsum launched Gyproc SoundBloc Infinaé 100 (UK's first 100% recycled plasterboard, Jan 2025). Etex/Siniat has RECYPLAC (100% recycled, Jul 2025). Strong EPD infrastructure among top-3 producers who dominate ~90% of market. Smaller regional producers may lack EPDs — similar split to other families.
structuralIssues.source: Knauf EPDs (https://www.environdec.com/); Saint-Gobain/Gyproc (https://www.gyproc.co.uk/sustainability); British Gypsum (https://www.british-gypsum.com/); IBU (https://ibu-epd.com/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document Big-3 EPD readiness in content. Note 100% recycled products as DPP exemplars.

structuralIssues.title: FGD gypsum supply declining as coal plants close — recycled gypsum becoming critical raw material
structuralIssues.description: More than 50% of German gypsum demand (~4Mt/yr) is met by FGD gypsum from coal power stations. European coal phase-out (Germany target 2038) will sharply reduce FGD supply. Plasterboard plants built adjacent to power stations will become 'orphans'. Raw material transition: FGD synthetic → recycled C&D gypsum + natural quarrying. Recycled gypsum market projected $3.21B by 2030. For DPP: raw material source (natural, FGD, recycled) is an emerging data attribute. EN 14190 (reprocessed gypsum) already provides the hEN framework. Siniat RECYPLAC (100% recycled) and Etex (35%+ recycled content) lead the transition.
structuralIssues.source: Clean Energy Wire (https://www.cleanenergywire.org/news/germanys-gypsum-supply-threatened-coal-exit-report); Global Gypsum (https://www.globalgypsum.com/magazine/the-last-word/744-often-overlooked-gypsum-is-the-wonder-material-we-cannot-do-without); ZKG (https://www.zkg.de/en/artikel/zkg_Latest_market_trends_for_FGD_gypsum-3749442.html)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document FGD supply transition in content.key_risks. Flag raw material source as DPP data attribute.

structuralIssues.title: European plasterboard $7.1B (2023), CAGR 7.09% — mid-sized CPR family dominated by Big-3
structuralIssues.description: European gypsum plasterboard market $7.10B (2023), growing to $14.08B by 2033 (CAGR 7.09%). Dominated by Saint-Gobain (Gyproc, British Gypsum, Rigips), Knauf, and Etex (Siniat, Fermacell). Growth drivers: EPBD renovation wave, fire safety (A1/A2 classification), acoustic performance, modular construction. Emerging alternatives: hempcrete (Hemspan), bio-based panels (Adaptavate). Single-TC structure (CEN/TC 241) and A2-s1,d0 CWFT classification simplify DPP implementation compared to multi-TC families.
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context in content for DPP impact assessment.
